dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal interpreted as trend-following breakout strategy. Entry uses pivot resistance (r1) as 'key chart resistance' proxy with volume confirmation. 'Second bar holding above' translated to close > r1 condition. Exit logic captures both time-based (2-day max hold via hold_days_max) and momentum-stall conditions (volume surge + price stall via close < open). Stop loss set to 1.5% per signal specification. Chart resistance levels are commonly identified via pivot points in systematic trading.

source_rule_text: Enter early-stage breakouts before the crowd arrives, and exit into the subsequent wave of late-arriving uninformed buying rather than hold for major moves.
